q-oni-oniart142-d5cfcfd2-817324cd.stem: According to ONI art. 142, which ordinance do the definitions of "risk analysis", "safety report" and "duty of diligence" refer to?
choices: To the ordinance of 14 March 1994 on the construction of vessels (OCEB).
To the Inland Navigation Ordinance (ONI) itself.
To the Navigation Regulations for Lake Geneva (RNL).
q-oni-oniart142-d5cfcfd2-817324cd.explanation: ONI art. 142 refers, for these concepts, to the articles of the OCEB (ordinance of 14 March 1994 on the construction of vessels).

q-oni-oniart142-d5cfcfd2-aefac3d2.stem: Among these statements about the definitions in ONI art. 142, which one is FALSE?
choices: The "duty of diligence" is defined in ONI art. 142 without reference to another ordinance.
The "safety report" is a report within the meaning of art. 2, let. e, and art. 17, para. 3, OCEB.
The "expert examination report" is a report within the meaning of art. 2, let. f, OCEB.
q-oni-oniart142-d5cfcfd2-aefac3d2.explanation: According to ONI art. 142, let. e, the "duty of diligence" is defined by reference to art. 5 OCEB, and not without reference.

q-oni-oniart142-d5cfcfd2-afaf3463.stem: According to ONI art. 142, how are the "experts" defined within the meaning of the special provisions of this section?
choices: As the persons referred to in art. 5a, para. 1, let. a, OCEB.
As the officers of the competent authority of the place of mooring.
As the members of a recognized classification society.
q-oni-oniart142-d5cfcfd2-afaf3463.explanation: According to ONI art. 142, let. c, the "experts" are the persons referred to in art. 5a, para. 1, let. a, OCEB.

q-oni-oniart2-2bf27aad-01b54b7e.stem: Within the meaning of the ONI, what does the term "motorized vessel" designate?
choices: A vessel with mechanical propulsion
A non-propelled vessel towed by another vessel
A floating device such as a dredger or a crane
q-oni-oniart2-2bf27aad-01b54b7e.explanation: ONI art. 2: a "motorized vessel" is a vessel with mechanical propulsion.

q-rnl-rnlart71-ba2d9252-53949420.stem: Among these statements about restricted visibility within the meaning of RNL art. 71, which one is FALSE?
choices: Navigation in restricted visibility applies only to navigation at night.
Navigation in snowy weather is considered navigation in restricted visibility.
Navigation in drizzly weather is considered navigation in restricted visibility.
q-rnl-rnlart71-ba2d9252-53949420.explanation: RNL art. 71 states that restricted visibility applies both by day and by night, and not only at night.

q-rnl-rnlart71-ba2d9252-81aaa7b5.stem: According to RNL art. 71, does navigation in restricted visibility also concern situations that are not explicitly listed?
choices: Yes, it also covers any other conditions limiting visibility in the same manner.
No, only the conditions specifically cited (mist, fog, etc.) are covered.
Yes, but only conditions caused by smoke from a fire.
q-rnl-rnlart71-ba2d9252-81aaa7b5.explanation: RNL art. 71 extends the concept to any other conditions limiting visibility in the same manner.

q-rnl-rnlart71-ba2d9252-b9f294ea.stem: According to RNL art. 71, what is considered navigation in restricted visibility?
choices: Any navigation, by day as by night, in mist, fog, drizzle, snow or heavy squalls with rain.
Only navigation at night in thick fog.
Only navigation by day when the rain is accompanied by strong wind.
q-rnl-rnlart71-ba2d9252-b9f294ea.explanation: RNL art. 71 defines restricted visibility as any navigation, by day as by night, in mist, fog, drizzle, snow or heavy squalls with rain.
